Name:
George Miller
aka. MC George, Son Of A Gun
Notes:
(??/??/2008) (George produces his first beat in GarageBand)
(??/??/2010) (George starts performing for "Beats N' Miso")
Release Date:
This era contains all of Joji's music before he started making music with the 
Pink Guy character. He first went by "MC George" in the video "Greatest Song Ever" before he later performed with the rap group Beats N' Baby Miso, which was when he started going by "MC Rukuss". In interviews, he stated that he started creating his first songs when he was 16, which would've been in 2008. He only ever released one song under the "MC George" name. He would also be in "BUSHIDO", which was uploaded to his friend's channel.

Name:
⭐️ i don't wanna waste my time [Mix 1]
(prod. Joji)
aka. waste my time
Notes:
OG Filename: IDWWMT.wav
Released on Joji’s SoundCloud account and later rereleased on "In Tongues (Deluxe).
In a Genius interview Joji shared the inspiration behind the song. "My friend was talking about cheating on his girl, and I was like, “I don’t know. You can do it if you want. Why are you talking to me?” And I thought about that a little more. Just, my friend cheating on his girl. And I wrote a song about that. It’s not about him, but it was inspired by that.

I started producing it first. It was a really hot day. I remember I was in my apartment
and the AC was broken, so it was very, very hot, and when I was piecing it together all I remember is the sweat from my face and armpits dripping onto my legs because it was a low couch. That was the setting. No AC, but that brings out more emotion sometimes.

Sometimes I'll already have the hook and then I build around that, but sometimes I’ll make the beat first. I love piano. I love organic instruments, so I’ll usually sample myself. I’ll go to the studio, play all the instruments possible there, and I’ll sample myself. Throw that on then add whatever beat matches to it. And that’s what I did on this song, then I added vocals. I already knew what the general lyrics were, but I did the beat first."

Release Date: “Lost Instruments” is a small collection of unreleased songs that was made in anticipation of Joji's debut EP "In Tongues". It was originally able to be streamed on WAV Media, a streaming platform intended to “give artists the support they need to succeed, while giving fans access to unique experiences they can’t get anywhere else” However, on December 22nd 2019, WAV Media was shut down, which meant that "Lost Instruments" was also taken down alongside the site.

Name: In Tongues (Deluxe)
Notes:
(11/03/2017) (In Tongues is released)
(02/14/2018) (In Tongues (Deluxe) is released)
Release Date: After the release of "In Tongues", a deluxe version was released the following year on Valentine's Day. The deluxe version included two tracks that previously released on Joji's SoundCloud account, along with eight remixes from various producers. However, the remix of "Window" that was sent in by Actress was the wrong one, and he actually sent an early version of his own song "Fire and Light". Actress tried to correct the mistake, but 88rising rejected it to ensure that the deluxe released on Valentine's Day.

Name:
⭐️ RL Grime - OMG
(feat. Joji & Chief Keef)
(prod. RL Grime, Diplo & Boaz van de Beatz)
Notes:
OG Filename: 06 OMG (feat. Joji & Chief Keef).wav
Track 9 on RL Grime’s album "NOVA".
It was originally a song by the band PRETTYMUCH, which they performed months before "OMG" released. It’s said that RL Grime played some beats to Chief Keef during a studio session and that he went with the same beat on "Traditions" despite it already been used. Joji was later offered a place on the song and many lines were changed like "Tradition for the squad" to "Donuts on the quad". Zion Kuwonu would later go live on Instagram and began showing a grudge against RL Grime for scrapping their song.

Name: Nectar
Notes:
(10/26/2018) (BALLADS 1 is released)
(09/25/2020) (Nectar is released)
Release Date: After the release of the "BALLADS 1" project, Joji would begin working with more and more producers and writers, and eventually leaving behind the lofi & trap sounds that many of us became used to over the years. There was lots of marketing in between the releases, which made the project very memorable as well. The project was announced on April 17th 2020, and was planned for release on July 10th 2020. However, on June 12th 2020, Joji announced that the album had been delayed to September 25th, 2020, citing the COVID-19 pandemic. A month after the project's release, Joji would do a paid virtual live stream titled "The Extravaganza" with live perfomances and comedic skits.

Kenny Beats & Joji - JOJI FREESTYLE
(prod. Kenny Beats & Joji)
aka. Yabba Dabba Doo*, Yabba Dabba Who?*, Quit Playin'
Notes:
OG Project Filename: JOJI FREESTYLE.als
In Episode 9 in Season 2 of Kenny Beats’ YouTube series "The Cave" Joji freestyles about the Flintstones and the child mortality rate during the stone age. He had other topics in mind for the freestyle, like falling in love with the Michelin Man, how Mewtwo doesn’t really look like a Pokémon, and what Lil Baby’s favorite desert could be.

Name:
⭐️ Gimme Love
(prod. Joji, The Donuts & Bēkon)
Notes:
OG Filename: JOJI - GIMME LOVE _rkMIX 6 - LL- master
The third single for "Nectar", which would be track 6 on the album.
In a promotional ad Joji stated "The song is essentially about losing track of important things and falling victim to time, it's not as neurotic as it sounds. Time goes by and we regret things, and everybody goes through this. The instrumental is fast and urgent but calm. People might relate to that feeling if they're in a world or environment that moves fast, so why not write a song about it?"

Name: SMITHEREENS
Notes:
(09/25/2020) (Nectar is released)
(06/26/2022) (SMITHEREENS is finished)
(11/04/2022) (SMITHEREENS is released)
Release Date: After the release of "Nectar" and after the "Nectar: The Finale" tour had concluded, there was hiatus from Joji for six months before the release of the lead single "Glimpse of Us" which leaked days prior to the song's announcement, with the "SMITHEREENS" album releasing a few months later. The project has two discs, the first disc includes songs that Joji only wrote on and sang, but the second disc starts off with "NIGHT RIDER" which switches up the flow of the project, going from melancholic ballads to a trap beat with electronic-like elements. Sadly, the project continues with more songs that Joji only wrote on and sang like "1AM FREESTYLE", with the exception of "YUKON (INTERLUDE)".

Name:
⭐️ Glimpse of Us
(prod. Connor McDonough) (writ. Connor McDonough, 
Riley McDonough, Idarose & CASTLE)
aka. Glimpse
Notes:
OG Filename: J_Glimpse of Us (JELLIS MIX)_BECKER MASTER
OG Filename (2): J_Glimpse Of Us (JELLIS MIX)_BECKER LP MASTER
The lead single for "SMITHEREENS" which would later be Track 1 on the album.
In an interview with Charlie Puth on SiriusXM, he was asked if there was a song that he regretted turning down, and he replied with Glimpse of Us, and said that it was actually sent to him. However, he was on tour at the time and he never got around to recording vocals for it, and two weeks later Joji released his version of it. All Joji did was sing it.

(prod. Joji & Isaac Sleator) (writ. Joji & Isaac Sleator)
Notes:
OG Filename: J_Yukon Interlude (SOMM TH MIX 1pt0)_BECKER LP MASTER
The second and last single for "SMITHEREENS" which would be Track 8 on the album.
It also interpolates "ATTENTION" from "BALLADS 1". The last two lines of the song are about how little creative freedom Joji has, which is obvious when six out of nine tracks on "SMITHEREENS" are songs he didn't write for or produce on at all, which is sad.

(prod. Joji) (writ. Joji)
aka. FUCK THE CLUB
Notes: Track 9 on Disc 2 on "Piss In The Wind (Deluxe)". First previewed back in May 2018 in a behind the scenes video for "YEAH RIGHT" with mumbled lyrics. Joji later dropped the song to YouTube under FTC / F*CK THE CLUB and cut the first part of the track. A demo for the song later surfaced with a finished verse and other little differences. The rights for the song were discovered by jae to have changed over to Virgin just before release.
